revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Isuzu River]][[fa:رود ایسوزو]][[ja:五十鈴川]]


{{Expand Japanese|topic=geo|五十鈴川|date=August 2012}} 
{{Infobox river
  | name              = Isuzu River
  | image             = Uji-bashi 01.JPG
  | image_caption     = People entering [[Ise Grand Shrine]] must cross the Isuzu River via the Uji Bridge
  | source1_location  = [[Mount Kamiji]]
  | mouth_location    = {{ill|Ise Bay|en|Ise Bay|qid=Q1368308|lt=Ise Bay|1=Ise Bay}}
  | subdivision_type1 = Country
  | subdivision_name1 = {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}
  | length            = 
  | source1_elevation =
  | discharge1_avg    = 
  | basin_size        = 
}}

The {{nihongo|'''Isuzu River'''|五十鈴川|''Isuzu-gawa''}} is a {{ill|River|en|River|qid=Q4022|lt=River|1=River}} that has both its source and its mouth in the city of {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.  The river is notable because it flows through [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and, due to its strong association with the Shrine, many {{ill|Traditional Japanese music|en|Traditional Japanese music|qid=Q7832297|lt=songs|1=Traditional Japanese music}} and {{ill|Japanese poetry|en|Japanese poetry|qid=Q1683305|lt=poems|1=Japanese poetry}} have been written about it throughout history.  The {{ill|Broken Stone in Uji Bridge|en|Broken Stone in Uji Bridge|qid=Q3387403|lt=Uji Bridge|1=Broken Stone in Uji Bridge}} serves as the entrance to Ise Grand Shrine, and crosses the Isuzu River. Vehicle manufacturer {{ill|Isuzu|en|Isuzu|qid=Q29803|lt=Isuzu|1=Isuzu}} is named after the river.

==References==
{{reflist}}

==External links==
{{coord|34.515682|136.743944|format=dms|display=inline,title|type:river_region:JP_scale:100000}} (confluence with Seta River)
